kinetic_proxy_models.substrate_mass_balance_g_per_h: d(V*S)/dt = beta0 + beta_feed * Fs - beta_our * max(60*OUR, 0)
kinetic_proxy_models.product_mass_balance_g_per_h: d(V*P)/dt = beta0 + beta_our * max(60*OUR, 0) + beta_paa * Fpaa - beta_deg * max(V*P, 0)
kinetic_proxy_models.respiration_rq: RQ residual = RQ - train-normal median RQ

notes: All residual centers, scales, kinetic proxy coefficients, and composite-score rules are defined from train_normal batches and prior process observability criteria.

The primary composite mechanism score uses substrate mass-balance and respiratory quotient residuals because they are dense online metabolic channels with direct biochemical interpretation and stable train-normal fit behavior.

Volume balance and product mass-balance residuals are retained as auxiliary diagnostic channels, but excluded from the primary composite score because normal batches show strong volume derivative spikes and the product proxy lacks dense biomass/growth-rate observability.
The visual threshold is the train-normal quantile and is used only for plots, not for model selection.
test_fault batches are used only for final diagnostic evaluation.

The substrate and product residuals are empirical fed-batch mass-balance residuals with train-normal fitted nonnegative proxy coefficients because exact feed concentrations and latent biomass are not directly available at dense sampling frequency.
